title: Top Notion Templates Every Small Business Needs to Succeed
author: TopStackTools Team
content:
Why Notion Templates Are a Game-Changer for Small Business

Running a small business means wearing every hat: sales, marketing, operations, finance, and customer service. You need systems that keep everything organized without burning hours on setup. Notion has become the default workspace for small teams because of its flexibility, but that same flexibility can be paralyzing when you open a blank page. Templates solve this problem by giving you a proven structure from day one.

We tested dozens of Notion templates across real small business workflows. Below are the ones that actually deliver results, organized by the business function they serve.

1. CRM and Sales Pipeline Template

Notion's database features make it a surprisingly capable CRM for businesses tracking up to a few hundred contacts. The best CRM templates include a contacts database with custom properties (deal stage, last contacted, deal value), a Kanban-style pipeline view, and linked meeting notes.

What makes Notion's approach unique is that every contact record can contain rich notes, embedded documents, and linked tasks. Unlike traditional CRMs where notes are plain text fields, Notion lets you build a full dossier on each prospect.

Best For

Solopreneurs and small teams (1-5 people) who manage fewer than 500 active contacts. If you need advanced automation, lead scoring, or email sequences built into your CRM, a dedicated tool like HubSpot CRM is a better fit.

2. Project Management Dashboard

This is the template category where Notion truly shines. A well-built project management template gives you Kanban boards for task status, timeline views for deadlines, and filtered views by team member or priority. The best ones include a master task database linked to a projects database, so you can see both the big picture and the details.

We found that Notion's project management templates work best when you resist the urge to over-customize. Start with the default views, use them for two weeks, and only then add complexity where you feel friction.

Recommended Setup

  • Master Tasks Database with properties: Status, Priority, Assignee, Due Date, Project
  • Projects Database with properties: Status, Timeline, Owner, Linked Tasks
  • Board View grouped by Status for daily work
  • Timeline View filtered by current quarter for planning

3. Content Calendar and Marketing Hub

If your business relies on content marketing, a Notion content calendar template is essential. The best versions track content from ideation through publishing, with properties for content type, target keyword, publish date, platform, and status. You can create filtered views showing only blog posts due this week, or social media content awaiting approval.

Some templates include a linked campaign database so you can group content pieces under specific marketing initiatives. This is especially useful for product launches or seasonal promotions where multiple content pieces need to ship on a coordinated schedule.

4. Meeting Notes and Decision Log

Meeting notes might seem trivial, but a structured template prevents the most common small business problem: decisions that get made in meetings and then forgotten. The best meeting notes templates auto-populate the date, link to attendees from a team database, and include a dedicated section for action items with assignees and due dates.

The real value comes from the decision log view. Filter your meeting notes database to show only entries with decisions recorded, and you have an instant audit trail of every important call your team has made.

5. Financial Tracking Dashboard

Notion is not accounting software and should not replace tools like QuickBooks or Xero. But a financial tracking template gives you quick visibility into revenue, expenses, and monthly burn rate without opening a separate app. The best templates include a transactions database with rollup properties that calculate monthly totals automatically.

This is particularly useful for freelancers and solopreneurs who want a single dashboard showing both project status and financial health side by side.

6. Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) Library

As your business grows, you need documented processes for recurring tasks. A Notion SOP template provides a structured format for writing step-by-step procedures, categorized by department or function. Each SOP links to the relevant tools, templates, and responsible team members.

This becomes invaluable when you hire your first employee or contractor. Instead of explaining everything verbally, you hand them a link to your SOP library.

7. Client Portal Template

Some Notion power users create client-facing portals where clients can see project status, review deliverables, and leave feedback. This requires sharing specific Notion pages externally, which Notion now supports natively. A well-structured client portal template includes a project overview, timeline, deliverables tracker, and feedback section.

The Limitation: When Notion Is Not Enough

Notion templates work brilliantly for organization and documentation, but they hit a ceiling when your business needs automation, payment processing, email marketing, sales funnels, or course delivery. Notion does not send emails. It does not process payments. It does not build landing pages.

This is where an all-in-one business platform fills the gap. Systeme.io combines funnels, email marketing, online courses, and affiliate management in a single platform with a genuinely usable free tier. While Notion organizes your internal operations, Systeme.io handles the customer-facing revenue engine: capturing leads, nurturing them with email sequences, and converting them through sales funnels.

The smartest approach for a small business in 2026 is to use both: Notion for internal organization and knowledge management, and a platform like Systeme.io for everything that touches revenue. This combination gives you enterprise-level structure at a fraction of the cost.

How to Get Started

  1. Pick one template that addresses your biggest pain point right now
  2. Use it for two weeks before customizing anything
  3. Add a second template once the first is part of your routine
  4. Link databases across templates to create a connected workspace
  5. Resist the urge to build everything at once — Notion overwhelm is real

The best Notion setup is the one you actually use consistently. Start simple, build habits, then expand.

title: Best AI Writing Tools in 2026: Tested and Ranked
author: TopStackTools Team
content:
AI Writing Tools Have Changed the Game

In 2026, AI writing tools are no longer novelties. They are production-grade content engines used by solo bloggers, marketing agencies, and Fortune 500 companies alike. The question is no longer whether to use AI for writing. The question is which tool fits your workflow, budget, and content goals.

We tested five of the most popular AI writing tools by generating the same types of content on each: a 1,500-word blog post, a product description, an email sequence, and social media copy. We evaluated output quality, ease of use, SEO capabilities, pricing, and unique strengths. Here are the results.

1. Jasper AI — Best Overall AI Writing Tool

Jasper remains the most well-rounded AI writing platform in 2026. It supports long-form blog posts, ad copy, email sequences, social media content, and product descriptions through a library of over 50 specialized templates. The Brand Voice feature lets you train Jasper on your existing content so outputs match your tone and style consistently.

What Sets Jasper Apart

  • Brand Voice training — upload existing content samples and Jasper learns your writing style
  • Campaign workflows — create an entire marketing campaign (ads, emails, landing page copy) from a single brief
  • Team collaboration — shared workspaces, approval flows, and content calendars for marketing teams
  • SEO mode — integrates with Surfer SEO for real-time content optimization
  • Chrome extension — use Jasper inside Gmail, Google Docs, and social platforms

Where Jasper Falls Short

Pricing starts at $49/month for the Creator plan (single user). The Teams plan at $125/month is where the collaboration features unlock. For solopreneurs on a tight budget, this is a significant investment. Output quality for highly technical or niche topics can still require heavy editing. Jasper works best when you provide detailed briefs and treat it as a first-draft generator rather than a finished-content machine.

Best For

Marketing teams and content-heavy businesses that publish frequently and need consistent brand voice across multiple channels.

2. Copy.ai — Best for Short-Form and Sales Copy

Copy.ai has carved out a strong position in short-form content generation. Its template library is optimized for high-conversion copy: product descriptions, Facebook ads, LinkedIn posts, cold emails, and landing page headlines. The free plan gives you 2,000 words per month, which is enough to test the platform thoroughly.

Strengths

  • Speed — generates multiple copy variations in seconds
  • Sales-focused templates — AIDA framework, PAS formula, and other proven copywriting structures built in
  • Workflow automation — connect to your CRM and generate personalized outreach at scale
  • Free plan — genuinely usable for small-volume needs

Limitations

Long-form content quality is noticeably weaker than Jasper. Copy.ai is built for punchy, conversion-focused copy rather than 2,000-word blog posts. If your primary need is SEO content, look elsewhere.

Best For

E-commerce businesses, sales teams, and marketers who need high-volume short-form copy.

3. Writesonic — Best Value for SEO Content

Writesonic positions itself as the affordable alternative to Jasper, and it delivers on that promise. The platform generates blog posts, landing pages, and ad copy with built-in SEO scoring. The Article Writer 6.0 feature produces structured long-form content with proper headings, internal linking suggestions, and keyword optimization.

Standout Features

  • Article Writer 6.0 — generates complete, structured blog posts from a keyword and outline
  • Built-in SEO scoring — real-time content grading without a third-party integration
  • Chatsonic — conversational AI assistant with web search capabilities for real-time research
  • Affordable pricing — starts at $16/month for 100,000 words

Limitations

Brand voice consistency is not as strong as Jasper. The interface has more features than it needs, which creates a learning curve. Template quality varies, and some outputs need significant editing for tone.

Best For

Budget-conscious content creators who need high-volume SEO articles without the Jasper price tag.

4. ChatGPT (GPT-4o) — Best for Versatility

ChatGPT is not a dedicated writing tool in the same way Jasper or Copy.ai are. It does not have built-in templates, SEO optimization, or brand voice training. But its raw language capability makes it the most versatile option on this list. With the right prompts, ChatGPT can produce blog posts, technical documentation, creative copy, scripts, and virtually any other text format.

Strengths

  • Flexibility — handles any writing task with the right prompt engineering
  • Cost — $20/month for GPT-4o access, or free with GPT-4o mini
  • Custom GPTs — build specialized writing assistants for specific content types
  • Code integration — useful for technical content that includes code snippets

Limitations

No built-in SEO tools. No brand voice memory across conversations (unless using Custom GPTs). No publishing integrations. You are responsible for formatting, optimization, and quality control. ChatGPT is a powerful engine, but you need to build the workflow around it yourself.

Best For

Technical writers, generalists, and people who want maximum flexibility and are comfortable with prompt engineering.

5. Rytr — Best Free AI Writer

Rytr offers the most generous free plan in the AI writing space: 10,000 characters per month with access to 40+ templates and 30+ languages. The paid plan at $9/month removes the character limit entirely. For solopreneurs who need occasional AI writing assistance without a major subscription commitment, Rytr is hard to beat on value.

Strengths

  • Free plan with meaningful output limits
  • Lowest paid tier of any major AI writing tool ($9/month)
  • Multi-language support — 30+ languages for international content
  • Simple interface — no learning curve

Limitations

Output quality for long-form content is a tier below Jasper and Writesonic. Limited customization options. No team features or advanced workflows. Rytr is a solid entry-level tool, but you will likely outgrow it if content becomes a core part of your business.

Best For

Beginners, hobbyist bloggers, and small businesses that need occasional AI writing at the lowest possible cost.

Using AI Writing Tools for SEO Content

One of the highest-value use cases for AI writing tools is generating SEO-optimized content at scale. The key is combining AI generation with proper keyword research, on-page optimization, and editorial oversight. Tools like Semrush handle the keyword research side, but the gap between keyword data and published content is where many businesses stall.

This is exactly the problem TopSEO was built to solve. TopSEO takes your target keyword, researches the competitive landscape, and generates a fully optimized article with proper heading structure, meta descriptions, and internal linking suggestions. It bridges the gap between SEO strategy and content execution, which is where most teams lose momentum.

If your content strategy depends on publishing 10 or more SEO articles per month, dedicated SEO content tools will outperform general-purpose AI writers every time.

How We Ranked These Tools

Our ranking considered five factors equally weighted:

  1. Output quality — readability, accuracy, and how much editing was required
  2. Ease of use — interface design, learning curve, and workflow efficiency
  3. SEO capability — built-in optimization, keyword targeting, and content scoring
  4. Pricing — value relative to output volume and feature set
  5. Unique strengths — what each tool does better than all the others

Our Recommendation

There is no single best AI writing tool for everyone. Jasper wins for marketing teams that need brand consistency. Copy.ai excels at short-form sales copy. Writesonic delivers the best value for high-volume SEO content. ChatGPT offers unmatched versatility. And Rytr is the best entry point for beginners.

For content marketers specifically focused on organic traffic growth, we recommend pairing a general AI writer with a dedicated SEO content tool like TopSEO. The AI writer handles variety (emails, social, ads), while TopSEO handles the blog content that drives long-term search traffic.

  • Best All-in-One Business Platforms for Solopreneurs in 2026
  • Best Email Marketing Tools for Small Business in 2026

title: How to Start Affiliate Marketing in 2026: Complete Beginner Guide
author: TopStackTools Team
content:
Why Affiliate Marketing Still Works in 2026

Affiliate marketing generated over $17 billion in revenue in 2025, and the industry continues to grow. The model is simple: you recommend products or services through unique tracking links, and you earn a commission when someone purchases through your link. No inventory. No customer support. No product development. You focus entirely on content and traffic.

What has changed in 2026 is the sophistication of the tools available to beginners. You no longer need coding skills, a large budget, or years of marketing experience to get started. Platforms now exist that give you everything you need — website, email marketing, sales funnels, and even built-in affiliate program management — for free.

This guide walks you through every step of starting an affiliate marketing business from scratch, with a focus on strategies that work right now.

Step 1: Choose a Profitable Niche

Your niche determines everything: the products you promote, the content you create, the audience you attract, and ultimately how much money you can make. The biggest mistake beginners make is choosing a niche based purely on personal interest without validating its commercial potential.

What Makes a Good Affiliate Niche in 2026

  • High buyer intent keywords exist — people are actively searching for product recommendations and comparisons
  • Recurring commission programs available — SaaS and subscription products pay you every month the customer stays subscribed
  • Sufficient search volume — enough people searching for your topics to build meaningful traffic
  • Manageable competition — you can realistically rank for long-tail keywords within 3-6 months

Best Affiliate Niches for Beginners

  • SaaS tools — business software, marketing tools, productivity apps (20-40% recurring commissions)
  • Online education — courses, coaching platforms, e-learning tools (15-50% commissions)
  • Web hosting and website builders — essential purchases with strong payouts ($50-200 per sale)
  • Personal finance tools — budgeting apps, investment platforms (high payouts, massive search volume)
  • Health and wellness — supplements, fitness programs, wellness apps (competitive but lucrative)

For this guide, we will use the SaaS tools niche as our example because it offers recurring commissions, high average order values, and evergreen content opportunities.

Step 2: Build Your Content Platform

You need a home base where you publish content and send traffic. For affiliate marketing in 2026, a website with a blog is still the highest-converting platform because search traffic has the strongest buyer intent of any traffic source.

Option A: Self-Hosted Website

Build a site using WordPress on Hostinger or a similar hosting provider. This gives you maximum control over design, SEO, and monetization. Cost: $3-10 per month for hosting.

Option B: All-in-One Platform (Recommended for Beginners)

This is where Systeme.io becomes the standout choice for new affiliate marketers. Systeme.io provides a free plan that includes website hosting, blog functionality, email marketing (up to 2,000 contacts), sales funnels, and — critically — built-in affiliate program management. This means you can both promote other people's products AND set up your own affiliate program for products you create later.

Most beginners burn their first months trying to connect five different tools: a website builder, an email service, a landing page tool, an analytics platform, and an affiliate tracking system. Systeme.io eliminates this entirely. You sign up for free, build your site, create your email list, and start publishing content the same day.

Why This Matters

The number one reason new affiliate marketers fail is not lack of knowledge. It is lack of momentum. They spend weeks choosing tools, configuring integrations, and troubleshooting technical issues instead of publishing content. The faster you start publishing, the faster Google indexes your pages, and the faster you start ranking.

Step 3: Create High-Converting Content

Not all content converts equally. In affiliate marketing, some content types dramatically outperform others because they capture people at the moment of purchase decision.

Highest-Converting Content Types

  1. Product comparisons — "Tool A vs Tool B" articles capture searchers actively choosing between options. These convert at 3-8% compared to 0.5-1% for general informational content.
  2. Best-of roundups — "Best [Category] Tools in 2026" articles attract people researching their options. These are the workhorses of affiliate content.
  3. Product reviews — In-depth, honest reviews build trust and rank well for branded search terms.
  4. How-to guides with tool recommendations — Teach something useful and naturally recommend tools within the tutorial. This is the content type you are reading right now.
  5. Alternative articles — "Best Alternatives to [Popular Tool]" captures people unhappy with their current solution and ready to switch.

Content Quality Standards

Google's helpful content update means thin affiliate content no longer ranks. Every article must provide genuine value beyond just listing products. Include:

  • Personal testing and hands-on experience
  • Specific pros and cons, not vague praise
  • Clear recommendations with reasoning
  • Honest mention of limitations
  • Comparison tables for quick scanning

Step 4: Master SEO for Affiliate Content

Search engine optimization is the engine that drives sustainable affiliate income. Unlike social media traffic that disappears when you stop posting, SEO traffic compounds over time. An article you publish today can generate commissions for years.

Keyword Research Strategy

Focus on long-tail keywords with commercial intent. Use tools like Semrush or free alternatives like Google Keyword Planner to find keywords like:

  • "best [tool category] for [audience]"
  • "[tool name] review 2026"
  • "[tool A] vs [tool B]"
  • "[tool name] alternatives"
  • "how to [task] with [tool]"

On-Page SEO Essentials

  • Target keyword in the title, H1, and first 100 words
  • Descriptive H2 and H3 headings that include related keywords
  • Internal links to your other relevant articles
  • Optimized meta description with a clear value proposition
  • Fast page load speed (under 3 seconds)
  • Mobile-responsive design

Step 5: Join the Right Affiliate Programs

Not all affiliate programs are created equal. Prioritize programs that offer:

  • Recurring commissions — you earn every month the customer stays subscribed, not just once
  • Long cookie duration — 30-90 day cookies give your content time to convert
  • Free trials or free plans — products with free entry points convert dramatically better because there is no risk for the buyer
  • Good product-market fit — only promote products you would genuinely recommend

Top Affiliate Programs for Beginners

  • Systeme.io — 60% lifetime recurring commissions, free plan makes it easy to convert, built-in affiliate dashboard
  • Hostinger — 60% commission per sale, strong brand recognition
  • Semrush — $200 per subscription sale, $10 per trial signup
  • HubSpot — 30% recurring commission for 12 months
  • ConvertKit — 30% recurring commission

Start with 3-5 programs maximum. It is better to create excellent content for a few products than mediocre content for dozens.

Step 6: Build an Email List from Day One

Your email list is the most valuable asset in affiliate marketing. Search rankings fluctuate. Social media algorithms change. But your email list is a direct communication channel you own.

Create a simple lead magnet related to your niche: a checklist, template, mini-guide, or resource list. Offer it in exchange for an email address. Use your email sequences to deliver value, build trust, and naturally recommend affiliate products.

With Systeme.io's free plan, you can manage up to 2,000 email contacts and set up automated email sequences at zero cost. This eliminates the need for a separate email marketing tool like ConvertKit or Mailchimp.

Step 7: Track, Optimize, and Scale

Once you have 20-30 published articles and a few months of data, patterns will emerge. Some articles will drive most of your affiliate clicks and commissions. Others will underperform.

What to Track

  • Which articles generate the most affiliate clicks
  • Which products have the highest conversion rates
  • Which keywords drive the most organic traffic
  • Average time from first visit to purchase (attribution window)

How to Scale

  • Create more content in your top-performing categories
  • Update and expand your best articles quarterly
  • Add new affiliate products that complement your existing recommendations
  • Build email funnels that nurture cold traffic into buyers
  • Consider using AI writing tools to increase your content publishing frequency

Realistic Income Timeline

Here is what to expect if you publish consistently:

  • Months 1-3: Building content inventory. Minimal traffic. Zero or near-zero income. This is the hardest phase.
  • Months 4-6: Articles begin ranking. First affiliate clicks. First commissions ($50-200/month).
  • Months 7-12: Content compounds. Traffic grows. Income scales ($500-2,000/month with consistent effort).
  • Year 2+: Established authority. Recurring commissions compound. $2,000-10,000+/month is achievable.

The key is consistency. Most people quit in months 2-3 because they see no results. The ones who keep publishing through that period are the ones who build real income.

Common Mistakes to Avoid

  • Promoting too many products — focus creates expertise and trust
  • Ignoring SEO — social media alone is not a sustainable traffic strategy
  • Writing thin content — 300-word product summaries do not rank or convert
  • Skipping email capture — every visitor who leaves without subscribing is a lost opportunity
  • Over-investing in tools — start with free or low-cost tools and upgrade only when revenue justifies it

Getting Started Today

The best time to start affiliate marketing was six months ago. The second best time is today. Here is your action plan for this week:

  1. Choose your niche (spend one day maximum on this decision)
  2. Sign up for a free Systeme.io account and build your basic site
  3. Write and publish your first comparison article
  4. Set up your email opt-in with a simple lead magnet
  5. Join 2-3 affiliate programs in your niche
  6. Publish your second article before the week ends

Momentum matters more than perfection. Start publishing, learn from the data, and improve as you go.

  • Best SEO Tools in 2026
  • Best AI Writing Tools in 2026: Tested and Ranked
description: Complete beginner guide to starting affiliate marketing in 2026. Learn niche selection, content strategy, SEO, email list building, and the best free tools to launch with zero upfront cost.

content:
Why Choosing the Right Course Platform Matters

The online education market crossed $400 billion in 2025, and individual creators are claiming a growing share of it. Whether you are a coach selling a signature program, a freelancer packaging expertise into a digital product, or a side-hustler looking for passive income, an online course platform is the engine that turns knowledge into revenue.

But picking the wrong platform can cost you hundreds per month before a single student enrolls. We built the same 6-module video course on Teachable, Thinkific, Kajabi, and Systeme.io to compare them side by side. Here is what we found.

What We Tested

Every platform was evaluated on five criteria that matter most to course creators:

  • Student experience — How does the course look and feel to the person taking it?
  • Marketing tools — Can you sell the course without bolting on extra software?
  • Pricing — Total cost in the first year, including transaction fees

Quick Comparison Table

Here is a snapshot before we dive into the details:

  • Systeme.io — $0/mo free plan, courses + funnels + email included, best value overall
  • Teachable — $39/mo, polished course player, 5% transaction fee on Basic
  • Thinkific — $36/mo, strong free plan for one course, no transaction fees
  • Kajabi — $149/mo, premium all-in-one, best for established brands

1. Systeme.io — Best Free Option (Our Top Pick)

Systeme.io is the only platform on this list that gives you a genuinely usable free plan with courses, sales funnels, email marketing, and automation included at zero cost. No credit card required, no 14-day trial clock ticking down.

What You Get on the Free Plan

  • 1 online course with unlimited students
  • 3 sales funnels with landing pages
  • 2,000 email contacts with unlimited sends
  • 1 automation workflow
  • 1 custom domain
  • Built-in payment processing via Stripe and PayPal
  • Zero transaction fees from Systeme.io

We had our test course live in under two hours, including the sales page and email confirmation sequence. The course builder is straightforward: create modules, add lessons (video, text, PDF), set drip schedules, and publish. No coding, no design skills needed.

Course Builder Strengths

The integrated approach is where Systeme.io separates itself. Your course, sales funnel, email list, and payment processing all live in one dashboard. When a student purchases, they are automatically enrolled, tagged in your email system, and entered into a post-purchase automation sequence. On other platforms, wiring up that workflow requires Zapier or manual configuration across multiple tools.

Video hosting is included. Upload directly to the platform without needing Vimeo or Wistia. Completion certificates are built in. Drip content scheduling works reliably.

Course Builder Limitations

The course player is functional but not as polished as Teachable or Kajabi. There are no community features, no discussion boards, and no mobile app for students. Video quality is compressed compared to dedicated hosts. Branding options for the course player are limited on the free plan.

For most creators selling a course under $500, these limitations are irrelevant. The student cares about the content, not whether the video player has a custom color scheme. But if you are selling a premium $2,000 program, the less polished interface may undermine the perceived value.

Total First-Year Cost

Free plan: $0. Startup plan ($27/mo) if you need more than 1 course: $324/year. Compare this to the $1,788 you would spend on Kajabi.

For a deeper look at everything the platform offers, read our full Systeme.io review.

2. Teachable — Best Polished Course Experience

Teachable has been a course-creator favorite since 2014, and for good reason. The course player is clean, professional, and distraction-free. Students can track progress, access lectures across devices, and the overall experience feels like a premium product.

Strengths

  • Best-in-class course player design

Weaknesses

The Hidden Cost Problem

Teachable looks affordable at $39/month, but add in the tools you will need: an email platform ($29/mo for ConvertKit), a landing page builder ($37/mo for Leadpages), and a payment tool for upsells. Suddenly you are at $105/month before your first student enrolls. Systeme.io gives you all of those features for free.

3. Thinkific — Best Free Plan for a Single Course

Weaknesses

The question is whether you need that level of polish on day one. For most people launching their first course, the answer is no. Start free, validate the idea, then upgrade if revenue justifies the cost. We wrote a full Systeme.io vs Kajabi comparison if you want a deeper breakdown.

How the Platforms Compare Head to Head

  • Best free plan: Systeme.io (courses + funnels + email at $0)

  • Cheapest to start: Systeme.io ($0) > Thinkific ($0 for 1 course) > Teachable ($39/mo) > Kajabi ($149/mo)
  • Best marketing tools: Systeme.io (free plan) > Kajabi (paid) > Teachable (requires add-ons) > Thinkific (requires add-ons)

Our Recommendation

For most people reading this article — especially first-time course creators, coaches, and solopreneurs — Systeme.io is the best place to start. The free plan gives you everything you need to build, market, and sell your first course without paying a single dollar in monthly fees. No other platform matches this value proposition.

If your course business is already generating $5,000 or more per month and you want a more premium student experience, upgrade to Kajabi. If you specifically need the best-looking course player and you are comfortable paying for separate email and funnel tools, choose Teachable. If you want a pure course platform with community features, Thinkific is worth considering.

But start free. Validate your idea. Get your first students. Then decide if you need to upgrade. The platform matters far less than the quality of your course content and your ability to reach the right audience.

title: Systeme.io vs Kajabi 2026: Full Comparison
description: A detailed head-to-head comparison of Systeme.io and Kajabi in 2026. Features, pricing, course builders, and marketing tools tested with real projects.
author: TopStackTools Team
content:
Two All-in-One Platforms, Radically Different Price Points

Systeme.io and Kajabi both promise the same thing: one platform to build courses, create funnels, send emails, and run your online business. Both deliver on that promise. The difference is that Kajabi charges $149/month to start, and Systeme.io has a free plan that covers the fundamentals.

That price gap raises the obvious question: is Kajabi worth 100x more, or is Systeme.io the smarter choice? We used both platforms for four weeks to build identical course funnels and find out exactly where each one excels and where it falls short.

Pricing: The Elephant in the Room

Systeme.io Plans (April 2026)

  • Free: $0/mo — 2,000 contacts, 3 funnels, 1 course, 1 automation, unlimited emails
  • Startup: $27/mo — 5,000 contacts, 10 funnels, 5 courses, 10 automations
  • Webinar: $47/mo — 10,000 contacts, 50 funnels, 20 courses, webinars
  • Unlimited: $97/mo — unlimited everything, priority support

Kajabi Plans (April 2026)

  • Basic: $149/mo — 10,000 contacts, 3 products, 3 funnels, 1 website
  • Growth: $199/mo — 25,000 contacts, 15 products, 15 funnels
  • Pro: $399/mo — 100,000 contacts, 100 products, 100 funnels

The numbers speak for themselves. Systeme.io's Unlimited plan at $97/month gives you more capacity than Kajabi's Basic plan at $149/month. And Systeme.io has a free plan. Kajabi has a 14-day trial, then you pay or lose access.

Over 12 months, the difference adds up fast: Systeme.io Startup costs $324/year. Kajabi Basic costs $1,788/year. That is $1,464 you could spend on advertising, content production, or simply keeping in your bank account while you grow.

Course Builder Comparison

Kajabi

Kajabi's course builder is excellent. Templates are polished, the student dashboard looks premium, and the content delivery options are extensive. You can create mini-courses, drip courses, coaching programs, and memberships. The course player supports video, audio, text, downloads, and assessments. Community features (discussion boards, live events) are built in on all plans.

Kajabi also offers a native mobile app. Students can download courses for offline access and track progress across devices. For premium courses priced at $500 or more, this polish helps justify the price tag to students.

Systeme.io

Systeme.io's course builder covers the essentials well. Create modules, add video and text lessons, set up drip schedules, issue completion certificates. Video hosting is included, so you do not need Vimeo or Wistia. The course player is clean and functional.

However, there are no community features, no discussion boards, no mobile app, and limited branding customization compared to Kajabi. The student experience is solid but not premium.

Verdict

Kajabi wins on course quality and student experience. If you sell courses above $500 and need a premium feel, Kajabi delivers. For courses priced at $50-300, Systeme.io's course builder is more than adequate and the money saved can be invested into marketing.

Sales Funnel Builder

Kajabi

Kajabi calls its funnels "Pipelines." The pipeline builder is visual, intuitive, and comes with proven templates for product launches, webinar funnels, and lead magnets. Each pipeline stage connects to email sequences and offers. The builder produces good-looking pages quickly.

Systeme.io

Systeme.io's funnel builder uses a block-based drag-and-drop editor with around 50 templates. It covers opt-in pages, sales pages, order forms, upsell pages, and thank-you pages. A/B testing is available on paid plans. One-click upsells and order bumps are built in.

The editor is less visually refined than Kajabi's but fully functional. We built a complete lead-magnet-to-sales-page funnel in about 45 minutes on Systeme.io versus 30 minutes on Kajabi. The extra time was mostly spent on design tweaks, not functionality.

Verdict

Kajabi is more polished, but Systeme.io is more generous. Systeme.io gives you 3 funnels free. Kajabi gives you 3 funnels on the $149/month Basic plan. Both platforms handle the core funnel workflow well.

Email Marketing

Kajabi

Kajabi's email marketing is above average. You get visual email templates, automation sequences, broadcast emails, and segmentation based on product ownership, tags, and behavior. Deliverability in our tests hit 96%, which is strong. Advanced features include conditional logic in automations and email A/B testing.

Systeme.io

Systeme.io includes email broadcasts, sequences, tagging, and a visual automation builder. Unlimited sends on every plan, including free. Deliverability in our tests was 94% — acceptable but not best-in-class. Segmentation is basic compared to Kajabi: you can tag and filter contacts, but there is no engagement scoring or advanced behavior-based segmentation.

For a detailed breakdown of email options, see our best email marketing tools guide.

Verdict

Kajabi has the edge in email sophistication. But for most solopreneurs sending newsletters and nurture sequences to a list under 5,000, Systeme.io's email tools are sufficient. The question is whether advanced segmentation is worth $149/month when basic segmentation is free.

Website Builder

Kajabi

Kajabi includes a full website builder with blog, custom pages, and professional templates. The sites look premium out of the box and are well-optimized for SEO. You can build a complete online presence without any other tools.

Systeme.io

Systeme.io offers basic website and blog functionality. You can create pages and publish blog posts, but the design options are limited compared to Kajabi. For a full website, you might want to pair Systeme.io with a dedicated site builder. For simple landing pages and a blog, it works fine.

Verdict

Kajabi wins clearly on website design quality. If your website is a major part of your brand, Kajabi's builder is significantly better. If your primary need is funnels and course delivery, Systeme.io's basic pages are adequate.

Automation and Workflows

Kajabi

Kajabi's automation builder supports multi-step workflows with conditional branching. Triggers include purchases, email opens, page visits, tag additions, and course completions. You can build complex customer journeys that respond to student behavior in real time.

Systeme.io

Systeme.io's automation is simpler. The free plan includes 1 workflow. Paid plans expand this to 10, and the Unlimited plan removes the cap. Triggers include form submissions, purchases, and tag events. The visual builder is easy to use but lacks conditional branching and advanced behavior tracking.

Verdict

Kajabi offers more powerful automation. For basic nurture sequences and purchase follow-ups, Systeme.io is fine. For complex multi-branch customer journeys, Kajabi is superior.

Who Should Choose Kajabi?

  • Established creators earning $5,000+ per month from digital products
  • Brands that need a premium look and feel across every touchpoint
  • Course creators selling programs at $500+ where a polished student experience matters
  • Businesses that need advanced automation and email segmentation
  • Creators who want community features integrated with their courses

Who Should Choose Systeme.io?

  • First-time course creators who want to validate an idea before spending money
  • Solopreneurs and coaches on a tight budget
  • Anyone who needs courses, funnels, and email in one place for free
  • Side-hustlers testing a digital product alongside a full-time job
  • Creators who prefer to invest marketing budget over software budget

Our Recommendation

Start with Systeme.io. Build your course, set up your funnel, grow your email list, and make your first sales — all without paying a monthly fee. If your business grows to a point where you need Kajabi's premium features and polish, you will know because your revenue will make the $149/month feel like a reasonable investment rather than a gamble.

The biggest mistake we see new course creators make is spending $150/month on a platform before they have a single student. That is $1,800 per year in fixed costs eating into an unvalidated idea. Systeme.io eliminates that risk entirely. Launch for free, prove the concept, then upgrade if needed.

title: How to Create a Sales Funnel for Free in 2026
description: A step-by-step guide to building a complete sales funnel without spending a dollar. We walk through the entire process using Systeme.io's free plan.
author: TopStackTools Team
ctaText: Build Your Free Funnel with Systeme.io
content:
You Do Not Need to Pay $147/Month to Build a Sales Funnel

The sales funnel industry has convinced a lot of people that building funnels requires expensive software. ClickFunnels charges $147/month. Leadpages starts at $37/month. Kartra costs $119/month. For someone just starting out, those numbers are hard to justify when you have not made your first sale yet.

Here is the truth: you can build a fully functional sales funnel for free in 2026. Not a stripped-down demo. Not a 14-day trial. A real funnel with a landing page, email automation, payment processing, and a delivery system — all at zero cost.

This guide walks you through the entire process using Systeme.io's free plan, which includes 3 sales funnels, 2,000 email contacts, unlimited email sends, and built-in payment processing. No credit card required to start.

What Is a Sales Funnel (And Why You Need One)

A sales funnel is the path someone follows from discovering you to buying from you. In its simplest form, it looks like this:

  1. Traffic source — Someone finds you (social media, Google, ads, referrals)
  2. Landing page — They land on a page designed to capture interest
  3. Lead capture — They give you their email in exchange for something valuable
  4. Nurture sequence — You build trust through a series of emails
  5. Sales page — You present your offer
  6. Purchase — They buy
  7. Delivery — They receive the product

Without a funnel, you are sending traffic to a generic website and hoping people figure out how to buy. A funnel guides them through each step deliberately. Businesses with sales funnels convert visitors at 3-5x the rate of those without one.

What You Will Build in This Guide

By the end of this tutorial, you will have:

  • A professional landing page that captures email addresses
  • A lead magnet delivery system
  • A 5-email nurture sequence that builds trust
  • A sales page with payment processing
  • A thank-you page with next steps
  • All of it connected and automated

Total cost: $0. Total time: approximately 2-3 hours.

Step 1: Create Your Systeme.io Account

Go to Systeme.io and sign up for the free plan. You will need an email address and a password. No credit card is required.

Once inside the dashboard, you will see the main navigation: Funnels, Emails, Courses, and more. For this guide, we are starting with Funnels.

The free plan gives you 3 funnels, which is enough to build a lead generation funnel, a sales funnel, and an upsell funnel. For your first funnel, one is all you need.

Step 2: Plan Your Funnel Before You Build

Before touching the builder, answer these four questions:

  1. Who is your ideal customer? Be specific. "Small business owners" is too broad. "Freelance graphic designers making $3,000-8,000/month who want to start selling templates" is useful.
  2. What free thing will you offer to capture their email? This is your lead magnet. It could be a checklist, template, mini-course, PDF guide, or resource list. The best lead magnets solve one specific problem quickly.
  3. What paid product will you offer? This could be a course, coaching package, template bundle, service, or digital product.
  4. What is the transformation you provide? People buy outcomes, not products. "A 30-page ebook about Instagram" sells poorly. "The exact posting system I used to grow from 500 to 10,000 Instagram followers in 90 days" sells much better.

Write these answers down. They will guide every word on your landing page and every email in your sequence.

Step 3: Build Your Landing Page

In the Systeme.io dashboard, click Funnels and then Create. Choose "Build an audience" as your funnel goal. Select a template — the cleaner, simpler templates tend to convert better. You can always customize later.

Landing Page Best Practices

  • Headline: State the benefit clearly. "Free Checklist: 27 Ways to Get More Clients Without Ads" works because it is specific and promises a clear outcome.
  • Subheadline: Expand on the headline with a supporting detail. "Used by 500+ freelancers to land their first $5K client."
  • Opt-in form: Keep it simple. Name and email is enough. Every additional field reduces conversions by 10-15%.
  • Social proof: Even basic proof helps. "Join 200+ subscribers" or a testimonial quote.
  • Remove distractions: No navigation menu, no sidebar, no links to other pages. The only action available should be entering an email address.

In the Systeme.io editor, click on any element to edit it. Drag blocks to rearrange. Use the settings panel on the right to change colors, fonts, and spacing. Save frequently.

Step 4: Create Your Thank-You Page

After someone submits their email, they land on the thank-you page. This page should:

  • Confirm their submission ("Check your inbox for the download link")
  • Set expectations for what comes next
  • Optionally introduce your paid offer — this is called a "tripwire" and it works because the visitor is in an action-taking mindset

In Systeme.io, the thank-you page is automatically created as the second step of your funnel. Click on it to edit the content.

Step 5: Set Up Your Email Sequence

Navigate to Emails in the dashboard and create a new campaign. This is where you build the nurture sequence that turns subscribers into customers.

The 5-Email Nurture Framework

Email 1 (Immediate): Deliver the lead magnet. Include the download link, a brief welcome, and set expectations. "Over the next few days, I am going to share the three biggest mistakes I see [your audience] make — and how to avoid them."

Email 2 (Day 2): Share your story. Why do you care about this topic? What experience do you have? Build a human connection. People buy from people they trust.

Email 3 (Day 4): Provide a valuable lesson. Teach something actionable related to your paid product. Give real value, not a teaser. This builds authority and reciprocity.

Email 4 (Day 6): Address the main objection. What stops people from buying? Price? Time? Skepticism? Address it head-on with evidence, testimonials, or logic.

Email 5 (Day 7): Present your offer. Explain what your product is, who it is for, and what outcome it delivers. Include a clear call to action linking to your sales page. Add urgency if genuine (limited spots, price increase, bonus expiring).

In Systeme.io, set the delay between each email using the automation settings. The free plan supports 1 automation workflow, which is exactly what you need for this sequence.

Step 6: Build Your Sales Page

Create a new funnel step or a separate funnel for your sales page. The sales page is where your paid offer lives.

Sales Page Structure That Converts

  1. Headline: State the primary outcome. "Learn to Build Sales Funnels That Convert — Even If You Have Never Built One Before."
  2. Problem section: Describe the pain point your audience faces. Make them feel understood.
  3. Solution section: Introduce your product as the solution. Focus on the transformation, not features.
  4. What is included: List the modules, templates, bonuses, or deliverables.
  5. Social proof: Testimonials, case studies, results. If you are just starting, use your own results or beta tester feedback.
  6. Pricing: Clear pricing with a strong call to action. Systeme.io integrates with Stripe and PayPal for payment processing at zero platform fees.
  7. FAQ: Answer the 5-6 most common objections as questions.
  8. Final call to action: Repeat the offer and the primary benefit.

Step 7: Connect Payment Processing

In your Systeme.io dashboard, go to Settings and connect your Stripe or PayPal account. This takes about five minutes. Once connected, you can add payment elements to any funnel page.

Systeme.io charges zero transaction fees. You only pay the standard Stripe fee (2.9% + $0.30 per transaction) or PayPal fee. This is a significant advantage over platforms like Teachable, which charges an additional 5% on its Basic plan.

Step 8: Connect Your Custom Domain (Optional)

The free plan includes 1 custom domain. Instead of your funnel living at yourbusiness.systeme.io, you can point your own domain to it. This improves credibility and brand consistency.

Go to Settings > Custom Domains and follow the DNS setup instructions. If you do not own a domain yet, you can skip this step and use the default Systeme.io subdomain while testing.

Step 9: Test Everything

Before sending any traffic, test the entire funnel yourself:

  • Submit the opt-in form with your own email
  • Verify the thank-you page loads correctly
  • Check that the lead magnet email arrives
  • Click through the email sequence (you can speed up delivery in test mode)
  • Test the sales page and payment process with Stripe test mode
  • Verify the purchase confirmation page and product delivery

Fix any broken links, typos, or formatting issues. A single broken step in your funnel can kill conversions.

Step 10: Drive Traffic to Your Funnel

Your funnel is live. Now it needs visitors. Here are free traffic strategies that work in 2026:

  • Content marketing: Write blog posts or create videos that solve problems your audience has. Link to your landing page within the content.
  • Social media: Share valuable content on the platforms where your audience spends time. Include your landing page link in your bio and in relevant posts.
  • Communities: Participate genuinely in Reddit, Facebook Groups, Discord servers, or forums related to your niche. When you provide value, people check your profile and find your funnel.
  • SEO: Optimize your landing page and content for search terms your audience uses. This takes time but builds free, compounding traffic. For help with SEO, see our best SEO tools guide.
  • Collaborations: Partner with creators in adjacent niches for cross-promotion, guest posts, or podcast appearances.

Common Mistakes to Avoid

  • Overcomplicating the funnel: Start with the simplest version. One landing page, one email sequence, one offer. You can add complexity after you have data.
  • Weak lead magnet: If nobody wants your free offer, the funnel stops at step one. Spend extra time making the lead magnet genuinely useful.
  • Selling too soon: The nurture sequence exists for a reason. Sending a sales pitch immediately after someone subscribes rarely works. Build trust first.
  • Ignoring mobile: Over 60% of traffic is mobile. Test your funnel on a phone before launching. Systeme.io templates are mobile-responsive, but always verify.
  • Not tracking results: Monitor your opt-in rate, email open rates, click rates, and sales conversion rate. Without data, you cannot improve.

What to Do After Your First Sale

Once your funnel produces its first sale, you have validated demand. Now you can:

  • Optimize: Improve the landing page headline, email subject lines, and sales page copy based on data
  • Scale: Increase traffic through paid ads, partnerships, or more content
  • Add an upsell: Create a second, higher-priced offer for customers who already bought
  • Upgrade your plan: If you need more funnels, contacts, or courses, Systeme.io's Startup plan at $27/month is the logical next step

Why We Recommend Systeme.io for Free Funnels

We have tested every major funnel builder and Systeme.io is the only one that lets you build a complete, production-ready sales funnel — including email automation and payment processing — on a permanently free plan. Not a trial. Not a limited demo. A real free plan that can generate real revenue. Read our full Systeme.io review for a detailed breakdown of every feature, or compare it to ClickFunnels if you are considering paid alternatives.

content:
Understanding the Sales Funnel Basics

Before we dive into building a sales funnel for free, let’s clarify what a sales funnel actually is. The sales funnel represents the customer journey from awareness to conversion, illustrating the steps potential customers take before making a purchase. For small business owners and solopreneurs, developing an effective sales funnel can mean the difference between stagnant sales and robust growth.

Step 1: Define Your Target Audience

Your sales funnel starts by understanding who you’re selling to. Take the time to create customer personas that capture the demographics, behaviors, and pain points of your ideal customers. Ask yourself:

  • What problems does my product solve?
  • Who experiences those problems?
  • What platforms do my customers use to seek solutions?

Having this information in hand allows you to craft a funnel that speaks directly to the needs of your potential customers.

Step 2: Create Awareness with Free Tools

At this stage, your goal is to attract visitors to your site. Content marketing tools like Semrush can help optimize your website content for search engines, making it easier for potential customers to find you. You don’t have to invest money; start with the free tools available, like Semrush’s free SEO toolkit.

Additionally, consider leveraging social media platforms to amplify your reach. Platforms like Facebook and Instagram allow you to post engaging content that directs attention to your website. Use eye-catching visuals and compelling captions that provide value to your followers.

Step 3: Capture Leads Effortlessly

Once potential customers are aware of you, the next step is capturing their information. This is where a tool like Systeme.io comes into play. It offers free options for creating landing pages that can collect email addresses in exchange for valuable content like eBooks or discounts.

Even if you don’t want to invest in paid software, you can use Google Forms or Mailchimp's free tier to create sign-up forms and email lists. Just make sure that your offering is enticing enough to encourage conversions.

Pros and Cons of Free Tools

  • Pros:
    • No financial investment is required.
    • Access to essential features to start building your funnel.
    • Good for testing different approaches to see what works.
  • Cons:
    • Limited functionality and features compared to paid versions.
    • May require additional effort to integrate tools.
    • Potentially limited customer support.

Step 4: Nurture Your Leads

After capturing leads, the next critical step is nurturing them until they're ready to convert. Email marketing is an effective way to stay in touch and provide value. You can use Mailchimp’s free tier or even HubSpot CRM, which offers a user-friendly interface for small businesses. They have automation features that allow you to send personalized emails to your leads based on their behavior.

Craft a series of emails that provide valuable content and gently push leads towards a purchase. Consider offering educational resources or updates about your products. This is also a great stage to leverage Jasper AI for creating persuasive email content quickly.

Step 5: Convert Your Leads into Customers

This is the moment you've been working towards! With your leads well-informed and interested, you can now guide them towards making a purchase. Utilize strong calls to action (CTAs) in your emails and on your website to encourage conversions.

Using Systeme.io, you can set up a simple and effective checkout process for your products. Make sure the process is as seamless as possible—any friction can lead to abandoned carts.

Analyze and Optimize Your Funnel

Once your funnel is established, don’t forget to analyze its effectiveness regularly. Use free analytics tools like Google Analytics to track user behavior and conversion rates. Identify where users drop off and adjust your funnel accordingly. Continuous optimization is key to improving performance.

Final Thoughts: Building a Free Sales Funnel

Building a sales funnel doesn’t have to be an expensive endeavor. With free tools and a solid understanding of your audience, you can create an effective funnel that drives sales. Start by defining your audience, capturing leads, nurturing them, and finally converting them into paying customers. Don't forget to regularly analyze your funnel’s performance for continuous improvement.

If you need robust tools down the line, consider integrating more advanced options or upgrading your current tools. Remember, the goal is to create a sustainable sales process that grows with your business.

Recommendation

For small business owners and solopreneurs looking to build an effective sales funnel, I recommend starting with Systeme.io for funnel creation since it combines the necessary elements in one platform. For broader marketing efforts, Semrush for SEO and HubSpot CRM for lead management can provide invaluable support without breaking the bank. Test these tools and see which combination works best for you!

title: Best AI Agent Security Tools in 2026: Tested and Ranked
description: Autonomous AI agents need identity, access control, and trust layers. We tested the top agent security tools so you don't have to.
author: TopStackTools Team
content:
Why AI Agents Need Security Infrastructure in 2026

Autonomous AI agents are no longer research projects. They transact on-chain, execute tools, access databases, and make decisions without human oversight. But most agents ship with zero identity verification, no access control, and no audit trail.

NIST launched the AI Agent Standards Initiative in February 2026 specifically to address this gap. Microsoft followed with the Agent Governance Toolkit. The Cloud Security Alliance published the Agentic Trust Framework. The message is clear: agent security is now a requirement, not a nice-to-have.

We tested the leading solutions for securing autonomous agents. Here's what actually works.

What to Look For in Agent Security Tools

Before diving into specific tools, here's what matters:

  • Agent Identity — Can the tool verify who an agent is before granting access?
  • Least-Privilege Access — Does it enforce task-scoped permissions rather than blanket access?
  • Pre-Action Risk Scoring — Can it evaluate actions before they execute?
  • Memory State Verification — Does it protect against memory poisoning or drift?
  • Execution Integrity — Can it verify that tool calls executed correctly?
  • Audit Trail — Does it log everything for compliance and debugging?

1. EP AgentIAM — Best Overall for Production Agents

What it does: Five-pillar identity and access management built specifically for autonomous AI agents. Not retrofitted from human IAM — designed from the ground up for agents.

Key services:

  • NoLeak — Execution integrity verification. Checks that tool calls produce expected outcomes.
  • MemGuard — Memory state verification. Detects drift, poisoning, or corruption in agent memory.
  • RiskOracle — Pre-action risk scoring. Evaluates proposed actions before execution.
  • SecureExec — Tool execution security. Sandboxes and validates tool use.
  • FlowCore — Full orchestration pipeline. Combines all services into a single API call.

Why we like it: EP AgentIAM is the only tool we tested that treats agents as first-class security principals. Most competitors bolt agent support onto existing human IAM systems. EP was built for agents from day one.

Pricing: Pay-per-call via x402 micropayments or API key. No minimum commitment. FlowCore (full pipeline) costs $0.02 per call — less than a single API request to most LLMs.

Best for: Trading agents, orchestrators, any agent handling financial transactions or sensitive data.

Uptime: 99.9% since launch. 9 endpoints live on 402index.

2. NVIDIA NemoClaw / OpenShell

What it does: A control layer beneath the AI agent runtime that enforces policy at the action level.

Why we like it: Strong technical architecture. NVIDIA's backing means enterprise credibility. The OpenShell concept — a security layer below the agent runtime — is architecturally sound.

Limitations: Primarily designed for NVIDIA's ecosystem. Less portable than API-based solutions. Requires NVIDIA infrastructure.

Best for: Enterprise teams already running NVIDIA AI infrastructure.

3. Microsoft Agent Governance Toolkit

What it does: Open-source runtime security for AI agents. Provides guardrails, monitoring, and policy enforcement.

Why we like it: Open source, well-documented, backed by Microsoft Security. Good integration with Azure.

Limitations: Primarily Azure-focused. Governance-oriented rather than identity-first. More about what agents can't do than verifying what they should do.

Best for: Azure-based enterprise agent deployments.

4. Cloud Security Alliance Agentic Trust Framework

What it does: A governance specification applying Zero Trust principles to autonomous agents.

Why we like it: Strong conceptual framework. Industry-standard backing. Good for compliance-driven organizations.

Limitations: It's a framework, not a tool. You still need to implement the actual security controls yourself.

Best for: Security architects designing agent governance policies.

Production Ready✅✅✅❌ Spec only

Our Recommendation

For most teams building autonomous agents in 2026, EP AgentIAM is the fastest path to production-grade agent security. It's cloud-agnostic, pay-per-call, and covers the full stack: identity, access control, risk scoring, memory protection, and execution integrity.

title: Best Time Tracking Software in 2026: Freelancers to Teams

content:
Time Is Money — Track It

Freelancers undercharge by 20-40% because they don't track time accurately. Agencies lose margins on scope creep. Remote teams struggle with accountability. Time tracking solves all three problems.

Toggl is the most popular time tracker for a reason — dead-simple one-click tracking, powerful reports, and integrations with 100+ tools. The free plan supports up to 5 users with full tracking features.

Clockify offers unlimited users and unlimited tracking on the free plan. Timer, manual entry, calendar view, and basic reports. The most generous free time tracker available.

RescueTime runs in the background and automatically tracks how you spend time on your computer. Detailed reports show productive vs. distracted time. Focus sessions block distracting websites.

title: Best Database Tools for Non-Developers in 2026
description: You need a database but don't want to write SQL. The best no-code database platforms for tracking anything — inventory, CRM, projects, and more.
author: TopStackTools Team
content:
Spreadsheets Hit a Wall

Every growing business reaches a point where spreadsheets break — too many rows, too many linked sheets, too many people editing at once. No-code databases solve this without requiring a developer.

1. Airtable — Best Overall

Price: Free (1,000 records/base) / From $20/user/month

Airtable looks like a spreadsheet but works like a database. Linked records, views (grid, kanban, calendar, gallery), automations, and 1,000+ integrations. The default choice for no-code databases.

Best for: Teams tracking anything — CRM, inventory, projects, content calendars.

2. Notion Databases — Best for All-in-One

Price: Free (personal) / $10/user/month

Notion databases live alongside your notes, docs, and wikis. Relations between databases, rollups, and formulas. If you already use Notion for notes, adding databases is natural.

Best for: Notion users who want databases integrated into their existing workspace.

3. Baserow — Best Open Source

Price: Free (self-hosted) / From $5/user/month (cloud)

Baserow is the open-source Airtable alternative. Self-host for free with no limits, or use the cloud version. API-first design means developers can extend it while non-developers use the visual interface.

Best for: Teams that want Airtable-like features with full data ownership.

4. Google Sheets — Best for Simplicity

Price: Free

For simple databases under 10,000 rows, Google Sheets works. Add data validation, dropdowns, and conditional formatting to create a basic but functional database. Everyone already knows how to use it.

Best for: Simple tracking needs where collaboration matters more than database features.

5. NocoDB — Best Free Alternative

Price: Free (open source)

NocoDB turns any SQL database into a smart spreadsheet interface. Connect to existing MySQL, PostgreSQL, or SQL Server databases and get a no-code interface on top. Completely free and open source.

Best for: Teams with existing databases that want a user-friendly interface layer.
